burial.plot_notes: Old St. Andrew's Church Cemetery, 59 Tariffville Road, Bloomfield CT.

parents.father_research_notes: John D. Stout of Hartford CT.

marriages.officiant: Rev. Theodore Ainsworth Greene; Rev. Dr. Frank Lattimer Janeway

narrative_blocks.header: Princeton lawyer who chaired the United Way
narrative_blocks.body: John Deming Stout Jr. graduated Princeton in 1933, earned law degrees from UConn and NYU, and spent decades at Hartford National Bank. He chaired the Community Chest (now United Way) in 1953 and was Life Trustee of the Greater Hartford Boys and Girls Club. He and Frances ("Francie") sailed their 40-foot ketch Anne Bonny between New England and the Bahamas beginning in 1966, buying a house on Man-O-War Cay in the Abacos in 1972.

narrative_blocks.header: Sailed New England to the Abacos every year
narrative_blocks.body: John kept a home at Man-o-War Cay in the Bahamas Abacos and sailed there from New England most years through his life as a member of the Cruising Club of America. He was a Hartford banker and trust officer by profession, but the sea was his second life.

sources.label: John D. Stout Jr. obituary, Hartford Courant, April 1994

marriages.officiant: Rev. Theodore Ainsworth Greene and Rev. Dr. Frank Lattimer Janeway

sources.label: Frances (Stanley) Stout obituary, Hartford Courant, May 2009

burial.plot_notes: Old St. Andrew's Church Cemetery, 59 Tariffville Road, Bloomfield CT. Died at Caleb Hitchcock Health Center, Bloomfield.

institutions.institution_blurb: Attended The Oxford School before Ethel Walker.
Graduated Ethel Walker School (sister TD0073 Jeanne also attended).

narrative_blocks.header: In 1938 her divorced sister was her matron
narrative_blocks.body: At Frances's wedding on May 20, 1938, her sister Jeanne — "Mrs. Thomas F. Oakes of Bloomfield" — served as matron of honor. Jeanne would divorce Thomas Oakes within two years, move alone to Manhattan, and die in her apartment on December 12, 1942, at age 29.

narrative_blocks.header: Fifty years sailing the Bahamas on the Anne
narrative_blocks.body: Frances and her husband Jack Stout began cruising to the Bahamas in 1966 aboard their 40-foot ketch, Anne Bonny — a name with a touch of piracy. They bought a house on Man-O-War Cay in the Abacos in 1972 and named it too Anne Bonny, making "many intrepid Gulf Stream crossings for boat work and supplies." After Jack's death in 1994 she kept sailing to the Bahamas alone until 2006, when — as she said — "the stairs became a bit too steep."

narrative_blocks.header: Local and national recognition for her daffodil collection
narrative_blocks.body: A lifelong member of the Garden Club of Hartford, Frances received both local and national recognition for her remarkable daffodil collection. Her obituary notes she "was delighted, in this, her final season, by copious bouquets from her own planting." She was also a dedicated birdwatcher, skier, and tennis player.

marriages.officiant: Rev. J. Edward Elliott (First Congregational Church of Harwich)

sources.label: New York Times — Donna L. Merrill, Samuel Stout Wed (June 28, 1970)

narrative_blocks.header: Wed his Lake Forest classmate during Navy service
narrative_blocks.body: Samuel and Donna Louise Merrill both attended Lake Forest College in Illinois, where they met. They married at her hometown's First Congregational Church in Harwich, Massachusetts on June 28, 1970, while he was serving as a Navy radar man third class in California.

education.notes: Lake Forest, Illinois — where he met his wife Donna Merrill.

military_service.notes: Stationed in California at the time of his June 1970 marriage.

parents.father_research_notes: Father Donald Loomis Merrill, real estate broker in South Harwich, Massachusetts. Family descended from the Merrills who settled in Pittsfield in 1775. Not built into tree.
parents.mother_research_notes: Mother referred to as 'Mrs. Donald Loomis Merrill' in 1970 wedding announcement; given name not preserved.

marriages.officiant: Rev. J. Edward Elliott (First Congregational Church of Harwich)

education.notes: Lake Forest, Illinois — graduated; met husband Samuel Stout there.

burialCemetery.name: Saint Andrew's Episcopal Church Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Old St. Andrew's Church, 59 Tariffville Road, Bloomfield CT. Burial site of TD0053 Frances Stanley Stout and I00214 John Deming Stout Jr.

INST023.name: Hopkins Academy
INST023.notes: Hadley, Massachusetts academy founded from the bequest of Edward Hopkins — the same bequest behind Hopkins Grammar School, New Haven (INST001), but a DISTINCT institution. Roger Hooker Leavitt and Hart Leavitt are graduates; compiler flagged the distinction Stage 6Y-687.
